title: The Promotion

jokes.text: Could you sign my expense report? - No way, no how. Expense reports are a day-to-day item. That is Jim's now. I am exclusively big picture, epic.
jokes.setup: Employee asks boss to sign routine paperwork
jokes.punchline: Michael refuses because he's now 'exclusively big picture, epic' - avoiding mundane tasks with grandiose language
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael deflecting responsibility with pompous language. 'Epic' is perfectly absurd for expense reports.

jokes.text: Sign this. Where's the 'please'? We're not animals. Sign it. Not without a 'please.' Idiot.
jokes.setup: Dwight demands politeness from his new boss
jokes.punchline: The absurd standoff over basic courtesy, ending with Dwight calling Jim an idiot
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamics showing Dwight's petty power struggle with new authority structure

jokes.text: I would like to file a huge, enormous, massive complaint about the tallest guy in our office.
jokes.setup: Dwight wants to file a complaint
jokes.punchline: Uses excessive adjectives then identifies Jim only as 'the tallest guy' - awkwardly formal yet childish
jokes.explanation: Dwight's formal complaint language contrasted with petty descriptor is mildly amusing

jokes.text: Jim won't sign my expense report. That is not... Okay... - That is day-to-day. - No, that his huge. You're trying to trick me. Get out, get out, get out...
jokes.setup: Dwight tries to escalate the expense report to Michael
jokes.punchline: Michael's exasperated realization he's being manipulated, followed by frantic ejection
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Michael catching on to manipulation, though predictable

jokes.text: I'd like to lodge a complaint. - I'm sorry to hear about that. - Who is this about? - You! Just want to let you know I take complaints very seriously.
jokes.setup: Dwight lodges complaint with Jim
jokes.punchline: Jim professionally handles a complaint about himself with complete sincerity
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist logic with Jim maintaining professional demeanor while being complained about to himself

jokes.text: When did this happen? - One minute ago. - And how do you feel? - Angry. - All right. Did he hit you? - No. - Did you cry? - No. - Did you feel like crying? - No. I'm gonna write, 'held back tears.' Wait, stop writing that. That is not true. If you stop crying, I'll stop writing it.
jokes.setup: Jim conducts formal complaint interview about himself
jokes.punchline: Jim fabricates emotional details despite Dwight's denials, then gaslights him about crying
jokes.explanation: Masterful escalation of the absurd complaint premise with Jim's gaslighting twist being genuinely surprising and character-perfect

jokes.text: I deserved that promotion, not Jim.
jokes.setup: Dwight addresses camera in talking head
jokes.punchline: Direct statement of grievance sets up the following fantasy
jokes.explanation: Setup line rather than standalone joke, but necessary for what follows

jokes.text: Makes me want to put him in a triangle choke hold and force him down to the ground and just keep pressing and pressing. And then flip him over and then put him in a hammer lock. And he's gasping, he's panting. Every last breath. And the crowd is going crazy. I emerge victorious! $18,000 and a chance at the title.
jokes.setup: Dwight's frustration with Jim
jokes.punchline: Elaborate wrestling fantasy complete with prize money and title shot
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight escalation from office complaint to elaborate wrestling fantasy with specific prize details making it absurdly vivid

jokes.text: Jim wants you to keep it down.
jokes.setup: Dwight's loud wrestling fantasy
jokes.punchline: Jim's understated response to the elaborate violent fantasy
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan punctuation to Dwight's fantasy, Jim's casual authority deflating the drama

jokes.text: Can I talk to you in my office? Sure, but could I first talk to you in my office?
jokes.setup: Michael requests private meeting
jokes.punchline: Jim counters with identical request, highlighting co-manager absurdity
jokes.explanation: Clean setup-punchline showing the inherent awkwardness of the co-manager situation

jokes.text: Can I also be a boss? It doesn't take a genius to know that any organization thrives when it has two leaders. Go ahead, name a country that doesn't have two presidents. A boat that sets sail without two captains. Where would catholicism be without the popes?
jokes.setup: Dwight wants to also be boss
jokes.punchline: Series of confidently wrong examples (no country has two presidents, boats have one captain, Catholicism has one pope)
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dwight logic with escalating confidence in completely wrong examples. 'The popes' is perfect absurd pluralization.

jokes.text: Jimothy... That sounds weird. Are you okay with being called Jim?
jokes.setup: Michael tries formal name variation
jokes.punchline: Immediate self-awareness that 'Jimothy' sounds terrible
jokes.explanation: 'Jimothy' became a fan favorite moment - absurd name invention with immediate self-correction

jokes.text: At first, we were talking about introducing a line of toilet paper. And what part of the human body does one use toilet paper upon? So you draw a line from there to the other planets, and I think by the end, we all learned a little bit about how small we are.
jokes.setup: Michael defends yesterday's meeting topic
jokes.punchline: Connecting toilet paper to existential cosmic awareness through body parts
jokes.explanation: Michael's ability to make profound connections between mundane and cosmic is peak character writing

jokes.text: You know what eats a large amount of the day are naps. You go to sleep, it's light out, you wake up, it's dark. That's the whole day. Where did that day go? I have no idea.
jokes.setup: Discussion of time-wasting activities
jokes.punchline: Michael's confused observation about naps consuming entire days
jokes.explanation: Mild Michael observation that shows his confusion about basic concepts like sleep duration

jokes.text: No more meetings this week. - Really? - You just agree to that? - I can be very surprising.
jokes.setup: Jim suggests no more meetings
jokes.punchline: Michael's instant agreement surprises Jim, followed by Michael's proud self-assessment
jokes.explanation: Michael's unpredictable cooperation creates mild comedy through character subversion

jokes.text: What's going on? I need to fit into my wedding dress. However, I'm also pregnant.
jokes.setup: Someone asks what's going on
jokes.punchline: Pam matter-of-factly presents contradictory goals
jokes.explanation: Simple contradiction delivered with Pam's characteristic directness

jokes.text: It's a myth that women have to gain more than nine pounds in a pregnancy? Look at these actresses. Some of them lose weight.
jokes.setup: Pam's pregnancy weight concerns
jokes.punchline: Questionable medical advice based on celebrity observation
jokes.explanation: Mild joke about unrealistic celebrity pregnancy standards, somewhat predictable

jokes.text: Why doesn't Crate & Barrel let you register for a toaster full of cash?
jokes.setup: Discussion of wedding registries and wanting cash
jokes.punchline: Absurd product idea combining traditional wedding gift with cash desire
jokes.explanation: Clever combination of wedding registry tropes with cash desire, good visual absurdity

jokes.text: My cousin makes the most amazing, romantic birdhouse mailboxes. I know I shouldn't tell you, but you'll still be surprised.
jokes.setup: Phyllis offers wedding gift advice
jokes.punchline: 'Romantic birdhouse mailboxes' as a wedding gift category, plus spoiling her own surprise
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phyllis character moment with absurd craft project and self-defeating surprise spoiler

jokes.text: If you don't smell this, you're fired.
jokes.setup: Michael conducting product focus group
jokes.punchline: Threatening termination over smell participation
jokes.explanation: Typical Michael abuse of authority for trivial matters, moderately amusing

jokes.text: Everybody, I think that Jim has gone insane, because he thinks that my office is a conference room.
jokes.setup: Jim objects to Michael's conference room meeting
jokes.punchline: Michael reframes the spatial reality to make Jim seem crazy
jokes.explanation: Good Michael deflection tactic, turning his wrong into Jim's insanity

jokes.text: What do you mean by 'these people'? ... You don't need to go anywhere. Anything that Jim has to say to me, he can say in front of these types of people.
jokes.setup: Jim uses phrase 'these people' about employees
jokes.punchline: Michael calls out the phrase then immediately uses 'these types of people' - even worse
jokes.explanation: Great Michael moment showing his complete lack of self-awareness while criticizing others

jokes.text: I love rivalries... Michael or Jim, Paris or Nicole, Heidi or L.C. It's so much fun. But I guess if I'm really thinking about it and answering your question honestly, I'd have to go with L.C. Heidi's a bad friend, and her skin is terrible.
jokes.setup: Kelly comments on office power struggle
jokes.punchline: Shifts to reality TV analysis with detailed personal judgments
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kelly character beat, derailing office drama for reality TV priorities

jokes.text: Hey, David. You got Jim here. This is Michael, senior co-manager.
jokes.setup: Conference call introduction
jokes.punchline: Michael immediately adds 'senior' to his co-manager title
jokes.explanation: Typical Michael power grab, adding hierarchy where none exists

jokes.text: Are you texting me?
jokes.setup: Serious conference call about raises
jokes.punchline: Michael texting the boss during the call he's on
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Michael's inappropriate behavior during serious business

jokes.text: When I am irritated and I look at you, I don't see you anymore. All I can see is how big and gross the pores on your nose are.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining working relationship problems
jokes.punchline: Uncomfortably specific and gross physical observation
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor with Michael's inappropriate oversharing about physical disgust

jokes.text: Hey, why haven't we ever... We have.
jokes.setup: Michael starts nostalgic question
jokes.punchline: Jim's immediate, flat correction cuts off the sentiment
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy in Jim's deflation of Michael's attempted bonding moment

jokes.text: Like break in in the middle of the night and change the numbers on payroll? - We can do it during the day. It doesn't have to be that dramatic.
jokes.setup: Jim suggests quietly adding raises to paychecks
jokes.punchline: Michael imagines elaborate nighttime heist, Jim's matter-of-fact correction
jokes.explanation: Great contrast between Michael's dramatic imagination and Jim's practical reality

jokes.text: You use your brain too much. - Are you advocating that I use it less? - Sometimes the smartest people don't think at all. - You just came up with that. - As I was saying it.
jokes.setup: Michael criticizes Jim's analytical approach
jokes.punchline: Michael's anti-intellectual philosophy immediately undermined by his admission of making it up
jokes.explanation: Excellent Michael moment showing his pseudo-wisdom immediately deflated by honesty about improvising

jokes.text: Con: you look like a nerd. Pro: you get to share your list with the other nerds. Con: you unzip your pants, and you find that there's a calculator down there.
jokes.setup: Michael contributing to pros and cons list
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd anti-nerd items culminating in calculator genitals
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation with the calculator reveal being genuinely unexpected and visual

jokes.text: I've been studying Michael for years, and I've condensed what I've learned into this chart. How Michael spends his time. As you can see, we have procrastinating and distracting others, and this tiny sliver here is critical thinking. I made it bigger, so that you could see it.
jokes.setup: Jim presenting analysis of Michael
jokes.punchline: Pie chart showing minimal critical thinking, enlarged for visibility
jokes.explanation: Great visual gag with Jim's scientific approach to Michael's dysfunction, self-aware about the tiny sliver

jokes.text: In the memo line, I'm gonna write, 'To love's eternal glory.'
jokes.setup: Creed writing wedding check
jokes.punchline: Overwrought romantic memo line for cash gift
jokes.explanation: Typical Creed oddness with inappropriately grandiose language for mundane transaction

jokes.text: Wait till Monday. Is this what I've become? Materialistic? Shallow? I feel horrible. Look! 'Mrs. Pam Halpert!' That's the first time I've seen it in writing.
jokes.setup: Pam having moral crisis about accepting cash
jokes.punchline: Immediate distraction by excitement over married name
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Pam's guilt immediately overridden by wedding excitement

jokes.text: I can see it in your crusty little eyes that you are going to make everything perfect.
jokes.setup: Michael criticizing Jim's perfectionist tendencies
jokes.punchline: 'Crusty little eyes' as unnecessarily harsh physical descriptor
jokes.explanation: Mild cringe with Michael's unnecessary personal attack disguised as observation

jokes.text: You're not good at making tough decisions. At thinking out the options, and then stating that decision very clearly, especially when it's an unpopular decision. Here's a tough decision for you. You suck. You suck. Is that clear enough for you?
jokes.setup: Michael giving Jim management feedback
jokes.punchline: Turns constructive criticism into childish insult while claiming it demonstrates decision-making clarity
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Michael's inability to maintain professional criticism without devolving to playground insults

jokes.text: You're not good at making tough decisions. At thinking out the options, and then stating that decision very clearly, especially when it's an unpopular decision.
jokes.setup: Michael asks Jim to list his weaknesses
jokes.punchline: Jim gives constructive criticism that perfectly describes what they're failing to do right now
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor - Jim's critique of Michael describes exactly what they're struggling with in this scene

jokes.text: OK, Skippy, here we go. Why don't you run yourself out there and tell them?
jokes.setup: Michael sending Jim to deliver bad news
jokes.punchline: Condescending 'Skippy' nickname while delegating unpleasant task
jokes.explanation: Predictable Michael behavior, passing buck while adding insult

jokes.text: Thank you, Jim, for thinking that we're smart people. But I do not understand. If we're such smart adults, then why don't we get raises?
jokes.setup: Jim's attempt to treat employees as smart adults
jokes.punchline: Oscar's logical takedown of the condescending framing
jokes.explanation: Sharp logical response that exposes the condescension in Jim's 'compliment'

jokes.text: Excellent question, genius. 'Cause Jim wants to give the raises to his friends and the people he sleeps with.
jokes.setup: Oscar's legitimate question about raise logic
jokes.punchline: Michael's inflammatory accusation about favoritism and sexual relationships
jokes.explanation: Michael deflecting criticism by making inappropriate sexual accusations

jokes.text: How is that gonna repair Ryan's car?
jokes.setup: Discussion of smart adults and raises
jokes.punchline: Kelly's complete non-sequitur about Ryan's car repair needs
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kelly randomness, derailing serious conversation with personal Ryan concern

jokes.text: My kid needs shoes! You want to tell him he doesn't get shoes? Her kid needs shoes, Jim!
jokes.setup: Employees upset about no raises
jokes.punchline: Meredith's dramatic shoe declaration, Michael repeating it for emphasis
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Meredith's dramatic appeal and Michael's opportunistic amplification

jokes.text: Her kid needs shoes, Jim! What the hell?
jokes.setup: Meredith mentions her kid needing shoes
jokes.punchline: Michael dramatically amplifies the emotional appeal against Jim
jokes.explanation: Michael's theatrical outrage on behalf of others while contributing nothing constructive

jokes.text: I'm just gonna rewind and back off a bit.
jokes.setup: The announcement goes terribly wrong
jokes.punchline: Jim uses VCR metaphor to describe retreating from the situation
jokes.explanation: Good use of technology metaphor for social retreat. Shows Jim's analytical mind even in crisis, trying to methodically undo his mistake.

jokes.text: These are the people who will open their hearts to you. They all have heart-ons for you, and that is a gift.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to defuse tension with inspirational speech
jokes.punchline: 'Heart-ons' as inappropriate sexual pun in workplace context
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael inappropriate wordplay trying to be motivational but landing as crude

jokes.text: A man... Panama. - That's not how that goes.
jokes.setup: Michael attempting palindrome or word puzzle
jokes.punchline: Completely botched execution immediately corrected
jokes.explanation: Michael failing at basic wordplay with immediate correction highlighting the failure

jokes.text: Ryan, obviously the best. - Well, obviously. Too bad he's a temp, he doesn't count.
jokes.setup: Michael ranking employees for merit raises
jokes.punchline: Declares Ryan the best then immediately disqualifies him
jokes.explanation: Good Michael logic showing his favoritism immediately undermined by technicality

jokes.text: Each Boston Baked Bean represents half a percent raise, we each got 24 beans, so the idea is you place the bean on the picture of who you think deserv... Who's that? - He's not a part of this. - Just wanted to draw a picture of him.
jokes.setup: Jim explaining bean voting system
jokes.punchline: Michael drew random person's picture just for fun, not for the vote
jokes.explanation: Great Michael moment showing his complete inability to stay focused on task at hand

jokes.text: I will skip a turn. - You're gonna still have to play that bean.
jokes.setup: Bean placement voting system
jokes.punchline: Michael treating it like a board game with turn-skipping
jokes.explanation: Michael's game logic applied to serious workplace decision-making

jokes.text: If the people here were our founding fathers, the Revolutionary War would have been delayed ten years, because Stanley Washington was napping, Phyllis Hancock was still signing the declaration, Kevin Jefferson was distracted by a butterfly.
jokes.setup: Dwight criticizing coworkers' work ethic
jokes.punchline: Historical analogy with each coworker's specific character flaw applied to founding fathers
jokes.explanation: Brilliant extended metaphor perfectly matching each character's traits to historical figures. Creative, well-structured, and highly quotable.

jokes.text: Why aren't there any beans on this very old, frizzy-haired picture of me?
jokes.setup: Bean voting system results
jokes.punchline: Phyllis's brutal self-description while complaining about no votes
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating moment from Phyllis with accurate but harsh self-assessment

jokes.text: Is he as matronly as Phyllis?
jokes.setup: Dwight questioning Jim's qualifications
jokes.punchline: Using 'matronly' as a positive workplace qualification
jokes.explanation: Dwight's odd use of 'matronly' as workplace skill, mildly amusing character logic

jokes.text: Come on... Tick. Let's get him... Tock. Let's get Jim! Tick. And drag Jim out of his office... Tock. Take his keys away from him. Tick... That's a clock.
jokes.setup: Dwight rallying employees with dramatic speech
jokes.punchline: Kevin's literal observation that Dwight is just making clock sounds
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Dwight's dramatic moment by Kevin's literal-mindedness. Great contrast between their thinking styles.

jokes.text: What does a bean mean?
jokes.setup: Complex bean voting system
jokes.punchline: Kevin still fundamentally confused about the basic concept
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Kevin's persistent confusion, economical and character-appropriate

jokes.text: On a scale of one to ten, I'd say I'm about a four. - What are you usually? - Six. You? Usually a ten, but I'm feeling like a zero.
jokes.setup: Jim and Michael checking emotional states
jokes.punchline: Michael's admission of going from self-rated perfect to complete failure
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing both their struggles with the situation, Michael's typical extreme self-assessment

jokes.text: I used to have to do this part alone, and it was the worst.
jokes.setup: Michael and Jim sharing difficult management moment
jokes.punchline: Michael's rare moment of vulnerability about management challenges
jokes.explanation: Humanizing moment for Michael, showing rare genuine emotion about management burdens

jokes.text: I used to have to do this part alone, and it was the worst.
jokes.setup: Michael reflects on making difficult decisions
jokes.punchline: His rare moment of vulnerability about the burden of management
jokes.explanation: Rare genuine Michael moment - showing actual appreciation for Jim's partnership in difficult situations

jokes.text: You cannot just keep avoiding us like this!
jokes.setup: Jim and Michael hiding from angry employees
jokes.punchline: Pam's frustrated call-out of their avoidance behavior
jokes.explanation: Straightforward reaction to management cowardice, functional but not particularly funny

jokes.text: Would you guys rather have $100 now or $5,000 a year from now? - $100 now for sure. 'Cause you just give me $50 to cover the broker fee, I put in 100 of my own money as the gift...
jokes.setup: Creed offering investment opportunity at wedding
jokes.punchline: Classic get-rich-quick scheme with upfront fees at inappropriate time
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed moment showing his sketchy side-hustles intruding on personal moments

jokes.text: The guy has an algorithm to determine the winner of any given college basketball game.
jokes.setup: Creed explaining his investment opportunity
jokes.punchline: Pseudo-scientific gambling system presented as legitimate investment
jokes.explanation: Classic Creed scam logic using technical-sounding language for sports betting

jokes.text: Don't tell Jim.
jokes.setup: Creed pitching scheme to Jim and Pam
jokes.punchline: Telling Jim not to tell Jim while Jim is standing right there
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed moment showing his complete disconnect from reality in the most economical way possible

tmdb_overview: David Wallace comes bearing the news that not all the staffers will receive a raise this year. Jim pushes Dwight to the limit and causes him to form office alliances. And Pam decides she wants only cash as a wedding gift.
